summary: Iter-79 Batch B (#4 Restaurant ON/OFF/auto + capacity gate + closed-banner/popup, #5 location-aware Contact) tested end-to-end. Backend: 14/14 pytest in /app/backend/tests/test_iter79_restaurant_hours.py — all green. Validated: (1) GET /api/restaurant/status returns full payload {open,reason,next_open_at,opens_in_minutes,open_time,close_time,mode,closed_message}; opens_in_minutes/next_open_at non-null when closed. (2) POST /api/admin/restaurant/hours admin-only (non-admin → 403, invalid time range → 400, invalid mode → 422). (3) Setting mode=manual_off ⇒ status.open=false reason='manual_off'; mode=manual_on ⇒ open=true reason=null. (4) GET /api/admin/restaurant/hours returns config + current_hourly_order_count + nested status. (5) POST /api/restaurant/order with mode=manual_off returns HTTP 423 with detail={code,message,opens_in_minutes,next_open_at} — detail is a DICT as required. (6) GET /api/messes/nearby?lat=20.898&lng=77.746 returns closest_mess_id='efoodcare-amravati' with distance_km≈5.56 (within 4-8km band). (7) GET /api/geo/reverse?lat=20.898&lng=77.746 returns label='Pushpak Colony, Amravati · 444601' (no coord fallback regex match); for lat=0,lng=0 label is exactly empty string. (8) POST /api/auth/profile with a base64 data:image/jpeg payload returns 200 in <1s (face-check is now off-path via asyncio.create_task). Frontend (Playwright, mobile 414x896 + desktop 1280x900): (a) /restaurant — sticky amber chip data-testid='restaurant-closed-chip' shows 'Kitchen is currently closed · Kitchen opens in 8h 30m · Daily 10:00–22:00'; popup data-testid='restaurant-closed-popup' has headline 'Kitchen is currently closed', body 'We only deliver between our standard working hours', countdown 'Opens in 8h 30m', 'Got it' button data-testid='restaurant-closed-popup-ok' dismisses; after re-navigating to /menu then back, popup does NOT re-open (sessionStorage `efc_closed_popup_dismissed`) but chip stays — exactly as spec. (b) /contact with geo=20.898,77.746 → pill 'Your nearest branch: efoodcare · Amravati · 5.6 km away', rows Branch/Address/Phone (+91 91755 60211)/WhatsApp/Email (hello@efoodcare.in)/Branch Manager/FSSAI/Hours populated from /api/messes/nearby mess doc, OpenStreetMap iframe centred on 20.9379,77.7782. (c) /contact without geo permission → 'Showing default branch: efoodcare · Amravati' + hint 'Enable location to auto-pick your nearest branch.' (d) /admin/restaurant-hours as admin (cookie-based session) — page renders correctly with H2 'Working hours', live-status card (Closed — opens in 8h 26m, reason: outside_hours), 3-option mode picker (hours-mode-auto/manual_on/manual_off), 2 time inputs (10:00 AM / 10:00 PM), capacity number input, closed-message textarea, 'Save changes' button data-testid='hours-save-button' triggers toast 'Saved — restaurant is CLOSED' on click. Cleanup performed: restaurant_hours restored to mode='auto', open_time='10:00', close_time='22:00', capacity_per_hour=0, default closed_message; ephemeral session token deleted.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/restaurant_hours.py:_compute_status — when mode='auto' AND now_ist.time() == close_t exactly (e.g. 22:00:00), `open_t <= now < close_t` returns False so kitchen closes one second early. Minor edge case; acceptable since close_time is exclusive by spec.

routes/restaurant_hours.py:_hourly_order_count uses created_at>=now-60min as ISO string comparison. Works only because server.iso() produces sortable RFC3339 with same TZ; if anyone writes a non-UTC ISO string into restaurant_orders later, comparison silently breaks. Consider storing UTC datetime objects in Mongo and comparing as datetime.
routes/restaurant_hours.py:admin_get_hours allows franchise_owner read access but admin_set_hours (correctly) admin-only; documented behaviour but worth a one-line docstring comment.
frontend RestaurantClosedBanner: polls /api/restaurant/status every 60s and ticks countdown every 30s — two timers when one with a derived countdown would suffice; not a bug, just churn.
frontend Contact.jsx fallback waits ~4s for geolocation before showing default mess — UX could surface a 'Locating…' skeleton meanwhile (currently the user sees a blank for ~4s). Non-blocking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (4/4 UI checkpoints: closed-chip+popup+dismiss-persistence, contact-with-geo, contact-no-geo fallback, admin-restaurant-hours save toast)

test_credentials: Admin: phone 9876543210 via dev OTP — promoted to role=admin in users collection. For the admin UI test the session token was injected as a `session_token` cookie because the frontend uses cookie-based auth (axios withCredentials=true, NOT localStorage). The OTP endpoint is rate-limited (10/10min per phone), so subsequent UI runs may need a fresh phone or a direct user_sessions Mongo insert.
seed_data_creation: pytest fixtures create 2 ephemeral users via dev OTP (admin user reused phone 9876543210, subscriber user 9123456701) and promote/demote roles in Mongo. No mess docs or orders were created. restaurant_hours app_settings doc was mutated mid-test and restored to defaults (mode=auto, 10:00-22:00, capacity_per_hour=0, default closed_message) at module teardown AND once more after frontend UI run. Ephemeral admin user_sessions token deleted post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter79_restaurant_hours.py covers all iter-79 Batch B (#4 + #5) backend changes. Frontend auth uses cookie session_token (axios withCredentials), not Authorization header — when stubbing admin UI tests inject the cookie via context.add_cookies, not localStorage. dev_otp endpoint rate-limit is 10min/phone, so for repeat E2E test runs either use a different phone or insert directly into user_sessions. Admin Hours page testids: hours-mode-{auto,manual_on,manual_off}, hours-open-time, hours-close-time, hours-capacity, hours-closed-message, hours-save-button, hours-live-status, hours-current-orders. RestaurantClosedBanner testids: restaurant-closed-chip, restaurant-closed-popup, restaurant-closed-popup-ok; popup dismiss persists across navigation via sessionStorage key 'efc_closed_popup_dismissed'. Contact page renders branch fields from /api/messes/nearby (geo granted) or first mess from /api/messes (denied/timeout) — both flows verified.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All 14 backend tests + 4 frontend UI checkpoints pass cleanly. Note: profile-save timing is the most impressive change — from ~3-8s (synchronous Gemini face-check) to <1s by moving the check to asyncio.create_task background fire-and-forget.
